name: LLM Scraper
tagline: Extract structured data from any webpage using LLMs and Zod schemas
seoTitle: LLM Scraper: Structured Web Scraping with LLMs
metaDescription: LLM Scraper is a TypeScript library that extracts structured, type-safe data from any webpage using LLMs guided by a Zod or JSON schema and Playwright.
overview: LLM Scraper is a TypeScript library for pulling structured data out of any webpage. You define the shape of the data you want with a Zod or JSON schema, point it at a live page, and an LLM fills in the values. The result is fully typed, so you get the same fields you described in your schema.

It is built for developers who already work with Node and TypeScript and want to avoid writing brittle CSS selectors or XPath rules that break when a site changes its markup. Instead of describing how to find each value, you describe what you want, and the model reads the page for you.

Within the web scraping and crawling space, it sits on top of Playwright for loading pages and the Vercel AI SDK for talking to models, so it works with OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Groq, Ollama, and other supported providers.
features: Define output with Zod or plain JSON Schema and get full TypeScript type-safety
Works with GPT, Sonnet, Gemini, Llama, and Qwen model series through the Vercel AI SDK
Built on Playwright, so it drives a real browser to load pages
Six input formatting modes: html, raw_html, markdown, text, image, and custom
Stream partial objects as the model extracts them via the stream() method
Generate a reusable Playwright scraping script from a schema with generate()

gettingStarted.intro: Install the library with Playwright and Zod, pick an LLM provider, then run a scraper against a page.

steps.heading: Install dependencies
steps.body: Install the core library together with Zod and Playwright from npm.

steps.heading: Initialize an LLM provider
steps.body: Install a provider package and create a model instance. This example uses OpenAI.

steps.heading: Create a scraper and extract data
steps.body: Launch a browser, open a page, define a schema, and run the scraper to get typed data back.

useCases: Turn a list page, like Hacker News front page or a search results page, into a typed array of records
Collect product, listing, or article fields from sites that change their markup often and break selector-based scrapers
Stream large extractions and show partial results as the model fills in the schema
Generate a reusable Playwright script once, then run it repeatedly without calling the model each time
